steps.title: Understand vertical shifts
steps.content: When a function's graph shifts up by $k$ units, every output value increases by $k$. So if $y = f(x)$ shifts up 9 units, the new function becomes $y = f(x) + 9$.

steps.title: Apply the shift rule
steps.content:
Substitute $f(x) = 3x^2$ into the transformation rule:
$$g(x) = f(x) + 9 = 3x^2 + 9$$
That confirms A is correct.
